commit.message:
Fix duplicate client/operation names when operation IDs contain multiple underscores (#5348)

* Initial plan

* Fix duplicate class/method generation when operation IDs contain multiple underscores

Use the first underscore as the separator in MultipleClientsFromOperationIdOperationNameGenerator:
- GetClientName: everything before the first '_' = client name
- GetOperationName: everything after the first '_' = operation name

This guarantees uniqueness since OpenAPI requires globally unique operation IDs.
Previously, 'Orders_items_get' and 'Products_items_get' both produced
client='items', operation='get', causing duplicate generated code.

fix: measure ping via TCP connect instead of full RPC round-trip

Previously ping_ms timed the entire health_check() call (HTTP connect
+ JSON encode + server processing + response decode), inflating the
displayed latency. Now a raw TCP connect to the RPC port is used for
the ping measurement, and health_check() is called separately only if
the TCP probe succeeds. This gives true network round-trip time.

Agent: Target State Alignment (#7547)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* Migrate dominantLineEnding from lint/refactor to @gml-modules/core

Boundary violation: `dominantLineEnding` was defined separately in
`lint/rule-helpers.ts` and `refactor/.../loop-length-hoisting-codemod.ts`
with divergent implementations. Per target-state.md §2.1, shared text
utilities belong in `@gml-modules/core`.

- Add `dominantLineEnding(text: string): "\r\n" | "\n"` to
  `src/core/src/utils/line-breaks.ts` with the correct count-based
  implementation (CRLF count vs bare-LF count; tie defaults to LF).
- Remove the local definition from `lint/rule-helpers.ts`.
- Update 8 lint rules that imported it to use `Core.dominantLineEnding`.
- Update the refactor loop-length-hoisting codemod to use the shared
  Core implementation (also corrects behaviour: was existence-based,
  now count-based, matching the rest of the codebase).
- Add 6 unit tests in `src/core/test/line-breaks.test.ts`.
- Add `dominant-line-ending-boundary.test.ts` in the lint workspace to
  catch future regression (verifies Core exports the function and
  rule-helpers.ts does not redefine it).

fix(api): update package_id on user profile when subscription plan changes

The subscription-updated webhook processor was updating paddle_price_id
on indb_payment_subscriptions but never resolving which package it
belonged to. The user's indb_auth_user_profiles.package_id stayed stale,
so the frontend still showed the old plan after upgrade/downgrade.

Now resolves the new package by scanning pricing_tiers for the matching
paddle_price_id and updates both indb_payment_subscriptions.package_id
and indb_auth_user_profiles.package_id.

commit.message:
Add interactive Governance Scorecard assessment tool

Adapt FSI-AgentGov's 6-phase interactive assessment for CopilotGov:
- 54 controls across 4 pillars (Readiness, Security, Compliance, Operations)
- Cumulative governance level model (Baseline/Recommended/Regulated)
  replaces AgentGov's zone-based model
- Inverted risk priority: baseline gaps weighted highest (most urgent)
- Multi-level score display with per-pillar breakdown
- Full export capabilities (JSON, Excel, CSV, print-to-PDF)
- All data stays in browser (localStorage), zero server dependencies

New files:
- docs/assessment/index.md - Scorecard page with SPA container
- docs/javascripts/assessment-app.js - Main SPA (~95KB)
- docs/javascripts/assessment-loader.js - Lazy loader
- docs/javascripts/assessment-data.json - Generated control data
- docs/javascripts/lib/chart.min.js - Vendored Chart.js 4.4.7
- docs/stylesheets/assessment.css - Assessment styles with dark mode
- scripts/extract_assessment_data.py - Data extraction from controls

Updated files:
- mkdocs.yml - Added extra_javascript, nav entry, exclude_docs
- .github/workflows/publish_docs.yml - Added data generation step
- .gitignore - Exclude generated assessment-data.json
- docs/start-here.md - Added scorecard scenario and tip callout
- docs/index.md - Added scorecard to Quick Navigation

All CI checks pass: verify_language_rules.py (0 violations),
verify_controls.py (0 errors), mkdocs build --strict (clean).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace `@template` with `@inheritParams` for shared roxygen documentation (#1066)

This fixes #814 with some behind-the-scenes cleanup that reduces the number of files and shortens the documentation section of others.

* Replace @template roxygen directives with @inheritParams r4ss_params

- Create R/params.R with internal r4ss_params() function documenting all
  shared parameters previously held in man-roxygen/ templates
- Replace all #' @template <name> lines across 102 R files with
  #' @inheritParams r4ss_params, collapsing multiple @template lines per
  roxygen block into a single @inheritParams line
- Handle special cases: mcmc.out.R and mcmc.nuisance.R use inline @param
  file with posteriors-specific docs; SS_fitbiasramp.R uses actual
  @references entry in place of @template methot2011cjfas
- Remove man-roxygen/ directory (no longer needed)

fix(ac): isolation responsabilités — watchdog, skills, system_prompts

watchdog scan_ac_issues(): supprime détections projet (Chromium, build no-op)
→ ne détecte que les bugs SF (.ts contient HTML = bug skill ac-codex)

ac-codex system_prompt + skill: règles extensions fichiers absolues
→ .ts = TypeScript only, HTML → index.html, docker_deploy() obligatoire
→ si deploy fail → corriger Dockerfile PROJET, jamais deploy/Dockerfile SF

ac-qa-agent system_prompt + skill: procédure récupération Chromium
→ Chromium absent → ajouter RUN npx playwright install dans Dockerfile PROJET
